findings.description: Inline re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", ...) recompiles/relies on the re cache each call instead of using a module-level precompiled pattern like the other two regexes; impact is a dict lookup, effectively negligible, and is a style-consistency nit rather than a real hot-path cost.

Per-line `line.strip()` is used only as an emptiness test but allocates a throwaway stripped string for every line; `line.isspace()`-style check would avoid the allocation, but the function is O(n) regardless and inputs are not large, so this is a micro-nit.
